measures.title: Programme graduate futures profiles completed and confirmed
measures.questionAnswered: Are agreed programme groups producing credible graduate futures profiles or equivalent story artefacts that programme teams recognise and can use?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of agreed programme groups with a completed Programme Graduate Futures Profile, reflection or equivalent story artefact, and count and percentage confirmed by the relevant programme team as accurate, usable and appropriately caveated.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with support from Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly during programme engagement, with annual synthesis
measures.baseline: 0 agreed programme groups have completed a profile, reflection or equivalent story artefact through this model.
measures.target: Target to be set once the agreed programme-group denominator is confirmed. The minimum expectation is that each agreed programme group completes and confirms a proportionate story artefact before wider use.

measures.title: Graduate futures stories used in practice
measures.questionAnswered: Are programme graduate futures stories being used in meaningful programme, student or applicant contexts?
measures.measure: Count of evidence-informed programme stories or claims used in programme review, recruitment, open days, advising, student-facing or applicant-facing contexts, together with examples of how they were used.
measures.owner: Programme teams and Heads of Department
measures.baseline: No programme stories have yet been produced or used through this model.
measures.target: Each completed and programme-confirmed story artefact has at least one agreed use or a recorded reason why visible use is not appropriate.

measures.title: Evidence is useful, fair and responsibly interpreted
measures.questionAnswered: Do programme teams find the evidence useful, fair and interpretable, and is it being used with appropriate caveats and academic judgement?
measures.measure: Structured feedback from programme teams and facilitators, plus sample review of profiles, conversations and story materials for clarity, caveat use, academic ownership, responsible interpretation and avoidance of ranking or punitive comparison.
measures.owner: Careers and Employability Service, Daniel Robson and programme teams
measures.cadence: During testing and programme engagement reviews
measures.baseline: No structured feedback or quality review has yet been completed through this model.
measures.target: Programme teams judge the evidence usable and fair; sampled materials are evidence-linked, appropriately caveated and free from ranking, league-table or punitive framing.

measures.title: Actionable enhancement insight generated
measures.questionAnswered: Are guided conversations and self-review producing useful questions, focus areas, support needs or local actions?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of completed guided conversations or enabled self-reviews that produce at least one agreed strength to amplify, question to investigate, support need, focus area or local action.
measures.owner: Heads of Department and faculty education leads
measures.baseline: 0 agreed programme groups have completed a guided conversation or enabled self-review through this model.
measures.target: Each completed conversation or self-review records at least one agreed strength, question, support need, focus area or action, while allowing a legitimate conclusion that no additional action is required.

measures.title: Enhancement insight routed and used
measures.questionAnswered: Are agreed programme and faculty insights entering normal planning and informing wider King's Edge work?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of agreed local actions or focus areas routed into programme, department or faculty planning, plus the number and nature of recurring themes, support needs or enhancement opportunities passed forward for King's-level synthesis through 2.4.1.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, faculty education leads and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly follow-up, with annual institutional synthesis
measures.baseline: No actions or themes have yet been routed through this model.
measures.target: Agreed actions and focus areas have a named local route and owner; recurring faculty-level themes suitable for 2.4.1 are logged and synthesised without ranking programmes.
